markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Close ED-WB-010 (operator full-parity order DEC-007 reverses the earlier descope): integrated terminal panel - bundled xterm-class frontend + Handshake backend PTY host (portable-pty or equivalent Rust crate; Windows ConPTY support REQUIRED), multiple terminal instances/tabs, cwd = workspace root, shell autodetect (pwsh/powershell/cmd), kill/restart, scrollback, copy/paste, EventLedger receipt per terminal session start/stop (attributable model/operator actions per HBR-SWARM). QUIET rules: terminals never pop OS windows or steal focus (in-app panel only).
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.
Bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries only (Tiptap/Monaco precedent); no CDN; no external app launches as product behavior.
PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; UI artifacts are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle (v02.193), product code, tests, and receipts.

OPERATOR ORDER (DEC-007, 2026-06-12): NO scaffolding, templates, mock-ups, or vibecoding; every feature must be working AND tested in runtime (executed vitest/Playwright/real-PG proof per feature, not status text). A feature without an executed runtime proof is NOT done.
PTY host is part of handshake_core (bundled library), not an external daemon.
Terminal sessions are recorded (start/stop receipts + attributed actor) for parallel-model observability.
No OS window spawning; in-app panel only.
scope.expected_behavior: Operator opens terminals inside Handshake, runs real commands in the workspace, multiple tabs, on Windows ConPTY.
acceptance_criteria: Real command execution round-trip proven in runtime (spawn pwsh, run echo + dir, assert output).
Kill/restart proven; no orphan processes (negative test).
Adversarial self-review recorded per the WP loop; findings fixed before evidence write.
verification: cargo test -p handshake_core --features runtime-full,test-utils (scoped) green with real-PG tests for backend state.
pnpm vitest run green; tsc --noEmit clean; eslint clean.
Offline Playwright runtime proof for each named capability (zero external network requests).
handoff_requirements: List files changed, tests run with exact names/counts, commits.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence and Playwright artifacts produced.
List remaining risks and any honestly-documented deviations (e.g., prose multi-cursor approach).

evidence.summary: Adversarial review found two MT-252 gaps after the initial implementation: real pre-authorization AiJob INTERACTIVE payloads collapsed interactiveAllowed to false, making Take-control unreachable, and exited HumanDev sessions still rendered as writable. Fixed in wtc-kernel-009 by separating request-capable interactive PTYs from interactiveAuthorized, emitting explicit Tauri interactiveAllowed, normalizing TS sessions from kind/exited state, forcing exited sessions read-only in TerminalPanel, and rejecting authorizeInteractive for capture sessions.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-252 builds an in-app integrated terminal panel (bundled xterm frontend + Handshake-native portable-pty/ConPTY backend, multi-tab, shell autodetect, kill/restart, EventLedger session receipts). The code implements the required behavior with real runtime proof: real PTY round-trip tests, concurrent-isolation tests, and a real-PostgreSQL EventLedger receipt-replay test that executed green. No placeholders, no forbidden deps, no self-cert.
findings: PTY backend is genuinely native: src/backend/handshake_core/src/terminal/pty.rs:70 imports portable_pty native_pty_system/CommandBuilder; pty.rs:3-4 documents ConPTY-on-Windows; Cargo.toml:246 declares portable-pty = "0.9.0". ConPTY teardown/EOF semantics handled in detail (pty.rs:217,309-366), not stubbed.

Real command round-trip proven: pty.rs:748 echo_round_trip_via_args spawns a real shell and asserts the echo marker in captured output; pty.rs:774-790 proves stdin keystroke echo; pty.rs:673 + windows_default_shell_resolver_prefers_pwsh_then_powershell_then_cmd proves pwsh/powershell/cmd autodetect (acceptance criterion 1).

Real-PG EventLedger receipts proven: tests/terminal_event_ledger_tests.rs:48 terminal_capture_session_receipts_land_in_postgres_event_ledger feeds a capture session then replays via the real db.list_kernel_events_for_aggregate("terminal_session", session_id) (storage/postgres.rs), asserting exactly 3 events FR-EVT-TERMINAL-SESSION-OPEN/COMMAND-EXEC/SESSION-CLOSE. The mirror is a real Handshake type (flight_recorder/event_ledger.rs:27,118 EventLedgerFlightRecorderMirror routing through the inner FlightRecorder + kernel append), not an implementer mock. lifecycle.evidence 2026-06-16T15:02 records it executed green (1 passed) against an isolated PG16 cluster with POSTGRES_TEST_URL set (acceptance criterion 3).

Kill/restart/no-orphan: pty.rs:508 kill() idempotent + Drop reaper (pty.rs:517-525 kill_on_drop + reader join), runtime.rs:1558 dropped_capture_sink_reaps_session and close_session kills the child (runtime.rs:563-589) (acceptance criterion 4).

Real IPC + bundled (non-CDN) frontend: app/src-tauri/src/commands/terminal.rs exposes #[tauri::command] kernel_terminal_create_session/write_stdin/resize/close/list/run_command/authorize_interactive; app/src/lib/ipc/terminal.ts:1 uses real @tauri-apps/api/core invoke; TerminalView.tsx:2-6 imports @xterm/xterm + addons; app/package.json:38-41 pins @xterm/xterm ^6.0.0 and fit/search/web-links addons (lockfile-governed, no CDN).

No placeholders/escapes (sub_rule_1): grep over runtime.rs, pty.rs, terminal.rs, TerminalPanel.tsx for todo!/unimplemented!/unreachable!/Unavailable/'not yet wired'/'deferred to follow'/'pending MT-'/mock/scaffold returned zero hits.
No forbidden deps: grep for sqlite/rusqlite/docker/elasticsearch/opensearch/meilisearch across the terminal impl returned zero hits; durable state is PostgreSQL/EventLedger only.

Adversarial-review findings (interactiveAllowed producer/consumer drift; writable exited HumanDev sessions) were found and fixed before the READY_FOR_VALIDATION promotion (lifecycle.evidence 2026-06-16T11:03 and 15:02), revalidated in Tauri IPC + frontend tests.

Aside (non-blocking): the validation PostgreSQL cluster was an ephemeral local cluster, not a long-lived service; the coder honestly flagged that the Integration Validator should rerun the ignored EventLedger test with their own POSTGRES_TEST_URL before final PASS. This is a normal handoff note, not a code defect.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger and/or offline Playwright where the contract names durable state or GUI behavior.
